question: Which stocks have the highest turnover (crs.) and do they show significant price movement as indicated by % Chng?

analysis.chart_type: Horizontal bar chart with color encoding
analysis.design_rationale: A horizontal bar chart is ideal for ranking stocks by their turnover, allowing viewers to immediately identify which stocks had the highest total traded value. By encoding % Chng as bar color, we reveal whether the most traded stocks also experienced significant price movements (positive or negative). Using color for % Chng highlights the relation between liquidity and volatility, making it easy to spot which highly traded stocks had large gains or losses.
analysis.target_insight: Viewer can quickly see the top turnover stocks and discern if their daily price change has been exceptionally high in either direction using color.

chart_encodings.other_aesthetics: Viridis color gradient for % Chng; sort by Turnover (crs.) descending; focus only on the top 15 stocks for clarity; all bar lengths shown; a colorbar to interpret % Chng values; gridlines for easier reading

transformations: Convert df['Turnover (crs.)'] from string to float after removing commas or formatting
Select top 15 rows for visual clarity
suggestion.matplotlib_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib as mpl
df = pd.read_csv("csv_c_squared\25.csv")
df["Turnover (crs.)"] = df["Turnover (crs.)"].str.replace(",", "").astype(float)
df_sorted = df.sort_values("Turnover (crs.)", ascending=False)
df_top = df_sorted.head(15)
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10,8))
cmap = mpl.colormaps["viridis"]
norm = mpl.colors.TwoSlopeNorm(vmin=df_top["% Chng"].min(), vcenter=0, vmax=df_top["% Chng"].max())
bars = ax.barh(df_top["Symbol"], df_top["Turnover (crs.)"], color=cmap(norm(df_top["% Chng"])) , edgecolor="black")
ax.invert_yaxis()
ax.set_xlabel("Turnover (crs.)", fontsize=12)
ax.set_ylabel("Symbol", fontsize=12)
ax.set_title("Top 15 NIFTY-50 Stocks by Turnover\nColor = % Change", fontsize=14)
sm = plt.cm.ScalarMappable(cmap=cmap, norm=norm)
sm.set_array([])
cbar = plt.colorbar(sm, ax=ax, pad=0.01)
cbar.set_label("% Chng", fontsize=12)
ax.grid(axis='x', linestyle='--', alpha=0.5)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
